en: Today's my birthday. Is today your birthday?
Happy birthday. Thanks. What'd you get?
My parents just gave me this. Isn't this the same desk? Yeah.
They gave me the same thing as last year. Oh.
ko: 응, 작년과 똑같은 걸 줬어. 아.

en: Oh. Maybe they thought you needed another one.
ko: 아마도 또 하나 필요하다고 생각했나 봐.

en: Maybe they weren't thinking about anything at all.
ko: 아마 아무것도 생각하지 않았던 것 같아.

en: The funny thing is about this is I didn't even like it the first time.
ko: 웃긴 건, 처음에 이걸 별로 좋아하지 않았다는 거야.

en: Todd, I think you're underestimating the value of this desk set.
ko: Todd, 이 책상 세트의 가치를 과소평가하고 있는 것 같아.

en: I mean, who would want a football or a baseball?
ko: 누가 축구공이나 야구공, 아니면 차를 원하겠어?

en: Or a car. Or a car.
If they could have a desk set as wonderful as this one.
ko: 이렇게 멋진 책상 세트를 가질 수 있다면 말이야.

en: I mean, if I were ever going to buy a desk set twice, I would probably buy this one both times.
ko: 만약 내가 책상 세트를 두 번 사야 한다면, 아마 이걸 살 거야.
